name: Banana Meringue Pie
recipeInstructions:
+------------------------------ FILLING ---------------------------------
+------------------------------ MERINGUE ---------------------------------
To make pastry: Combine flour, sugar and butter in a food processor. Process until the consistency of fine meal. Add beaten egg; continue to process just until dough starts to come together. Gather into a ball, wrap and refrigerate for one hour or place in freezer for 15 minutes to chill. Roll out dough on a floured surface or between two sheets of plastic wrap to an 11-inch circle. Dough is sticky and falls apart easily; just patch together and fit into a nine-inch flan pan with removable bottom; trim edges. (If you don't have a flan pan, a pie plate can be used.) Chill for 30 minutes.
Bake pastry at 375°F until light golden in color.
To make filling: Thinly slice five of the bananas and arrange on bottom of pastry shell. Mash the remaining banana and combine with warm cream, sugar, cornstarch and vanilla.
Pour over banana layer. Bake at 350°F for 35 - 40 minutes or until filling is set. Let pie cool slightly.
To make meringue: Beat egg whites with a pinch of salt until soft peaks form. Gradually beat in 1/3 cup sugar until meringue is stiff and glossy. Spread over warm pie; bake at 350°F for 12 - 15 minutes or until browned. Let cool; refrigerate before serving.
Serves 6.
From The Gazette, 91/02/13.
